title: Find area by counting unit squares
description: Find the area of shapes by counting the unit squares inside them.
topic_path: Find area by counting unit squares

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which shape below has an area of $12$ square units?**


[[☃ image 1]]

 is $1$ square unit.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The area is the number of $1 \times 1$ squares the shape covers.

 Let's count the number of squares covered in each shape to find which shape has an area of $12$ square units.

[[☃ image 1]]

This shape has an area of $11$ square units, not $12$ square units.

[[☃ image 1]]

This shape has an area of $10$ square units, not $12$ square units.

[[☃ image 1]]

This shape has an area of $12$ square units.

options.alt: A square is equal to one square unit.

choices.content: ![A shape with 8 sides made up of 11 squares is drawn on a grid. The length of each side is as follows, beginning at the top in a clockwise pattern, 4 squares to the right, 2 squares down, 2 squares to the left, 1 square down, 1 square to the left, 1 square down, 1 square to the left, 4 squares up. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/31a734eff007de3479b57d0c6e5dd3e409c70a1c)

![A shape with 8 sides is made up of 10 squares is drawn on a grid. The length of each side is as follows, beginning at the top in a clockwise pattern, 2 squares to the right, 3 squares down, 1 square to the left, 3 squares down, 2 squares to the left, 1 square up, 1 square to the right, 5 squares up.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2f70a51e52f03de22dd7157588e0f08ed74f3bd4)

![A shape with 4 sides is made up of 12 squares drawn on a grid. The length of each side is as follows, beginning at the top in a clockwise pattern, 6 squares to the right, 2 squares down, 6 squares to the left, 2 squares up.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fb2862b8230a56633949d781aa2e98a578e137eb)

assessment_items.question_content:
Each square in the grid is a $1 \times 1$ meter square.


[[☃ image 1]]

** What is the area of the shape? **

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] square meters
hints: The area is the number of $1 \times 1$ squares the shape covers.

Let's count the number of squares covered.

[[☃ image 1]]
The area is $10$ square meters.
